target.title: Fold Telegram RTT sampling into live QA evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Fold Telegram package RTT measurement into the normal Telegram live QA lane instead of maintaining a separate `pnpm rtt` command and Telegram-only RTT Docker driver.
- Emit repeated RTT timing through `qa-evidence.json` under `result.timing` for `telegram-mentioned-message-reply`, including avg/p50/p95/max/sample/failure counts, while clearing stale one-off `rttMs` when no repeated RTT checks pass.
- Use first-class package Telegram RTT controls: `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_SAMPLES`, `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_TIMEOUT_MS`, `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_MAX_FAILURES`, and `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_RTT_CHECKS`.
- Keep focused non-RTT scenario runs from forcing the default RTT check, pass injected env through Convex credential leasing, and make RTT-specific timeout authoritative for repeated RTT checks.
- Stop writing the duplicate Telegram observed-message sidecar from the live Telegram lane; mantis keeps a narrow legacy fallback for older artifact directories that still contain it, and docs now point to the current report/evidence artifacts.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/npm-telegram-live.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-build-telegram-evidence.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm oxfmt --check docs/concepts/qa-e2e-automation.md docs/concepts/mantis.md docs/help/testing.md extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-transport-result.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-transport-rtt.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.ts scripts/e2e/npm-telegram-live-docker.sh scripts/e2e/npm-telegram-live-runner.ts scripts/mantis/build-telegram-evidence.mjs test/scripts/mantis-build-telegram-evidence.test.ts test/scripts/npm-telegram-live.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt "..."` clean; legacy `OPENCLAW_NPM_TELEGRAM_WARM/SAMPLE/MAX_FAILURES` aliases intentionally out of scope per requested no-legacy end state and paired openclaw-rtt#15 update.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram package RTT measurement now runs through the standard package Telegram live QA lane and writes timing into `qa-evidence.json` instead of a separate RTT command/output shape.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on macOS, using focused unit/schema/script tests plus extension typecheck, docs, formatting, whitespace checks, and autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused evidence, Telegram live runtime, package Telegram live script, and mantis legacy import tests; ran extension typecheck; ran docs list, formatter check, whitespace check, and final autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 4 files / 72 tests; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm docs:list`, focused `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, and final autoreview passed.

Observed result after fix: Package Telegram RTT controls flow through package Telegram live, repeated RTT checks are summarized as evidence timing, focused non-RTT runs skip default RTT, and new Telegram live output no longer writes a duplicate observed-message sidecar. Legacy mantis import still tolerates older artifact directories that contain `telegram-qa-observed-messages.json`.

What was not tested: No live Telegram/Convex/Docker lane was executed in this local pass.
target.author: romneyda
